captured.evidence: Phase I involves oxidation, reduction, and hydrolysis that modify the drug and can produce active or toxic metabolites
Phase II is conjugation, mainly glucuronidation, making drugs water-soluble for excretion
CYP3A4 is the major CYP enzyme handling about half of drug metabolism including fentanyl and midazolam
tissue esterases hydrolyze it through ester hydrolysis, not CYP enzymes. Organ-independent metabolism keeps CSHT at 3-5 minutes no matter how long the infusion runs

missed.description: Halothane hepatitis mechanism via reductive metabolism and trifluoroacetyl chloride intermediates
Codeine as prodrug requiring CYP2D6 metabolism to morphine

summary: Solid grasp of drug metabolism phases and remifentanil's unique esterase pathway. Missing discussion of halothane hepatitis and codeine prodrug metabolism examples.
voss_quip: Good. Now explain why your patient won't need naloxone tomorrow.
